Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real merit. Marcus defended the cornmeal-sealing technique; Stephanie redirected toward the moisture-seal angle as the story. Margaret agreed, and Stephanie confirmed Marcus will anchor copy around heat arriving concentrated, not diffused.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should lead with crust performance, not ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three shots of the bite-through moment. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over the jalapeno cross-section Julian had championed. Shot two locked in unanimously.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - "stop explaining the why, just show what happens when you fry it." Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the copy to lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivot to texture. Margaret approved - send it.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - but the team had no notes. Marcus credited trusting the work over overthinking it. Stephanie noted the team listened rather than defended positions. Julian and Marcus agreed restraint drove both the shot and copy success.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it appears to be a technical deployment exchange. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the project live. Margaret suggested restraint as a guiding principle, which Devon agreed with. No ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were discussed.
